basic_details.job_title: UKSSSC Livestock Extension Officer Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 120 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Pashudhan Prasar Adhikari (Livestock Extension Officer), Group-C
basic_details.short_information: The Uttarakhand Subordinate Services Selection Commission (UKSSSC) has published the Pashudhan Prasar Adhikari (Livestock Extension Officer) Notification 2026 on 08 May 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 120 Livestock Extension Officer positions. Candidates within the age range of 21–42 years (with relaxations for SC/ST/OBC, Divyang, Ex-Servicemen, and other reserved categories) and holding a Bachelor’s Degree in Biology, Agriculture, or Animal Husbandry are eligible to apply online at www.sssc.uk.gov.in. The application process will be active from 13 May 2026 to 02 June 2026, and candid

application_fee.details: Payment mode: Credit Card / Debit Card / Net Banking / UPI only. Bank drafts and cheques are not accepted. Fee once paid is non-refundable. Application is considered complete only when fee reaches the Commission's account within the deadline.

age_limit.details: The decisive date (cut-off) for age calculation is 01 July 2026. Candidates must be minimum 21 years and not more than 42 years as on this date — i.e., born not after 01 July 2005 and not before 02 July 1984.

qualification.domicile: Permanent/Original Resident Certificate of Uttarakhand; AND Employment Office registration in Uttarakhand (mandatory for all candidates applying under Group-C direct recruitment outside UKPSC purview)
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (02-06-2026). The application is open to candidates outside the Uttarakhand Public Service Commission's purview. Candidates must be registered at an Employment Office in Uttarakhand or hold a valid Permanent/Original Resident Certificate of Uttarakhand. | Educational Qualification | Pre-Appointment Training Note: Selected candidates for Livestock Extension Officer will undergo 06 months training at the Departmental Training Centre and 06 months training at Departmental Veterinary Hospitals under the supervision of Veterinary Officers before being appointed to vacancies. During training, no salary, honorarium or allowances are payable. | Desirable / Additional Qualifications (Bonus Marks)

vacancy_details.Post / Department: Livestock Extension Officer (Animal Husbandry Dept.) – Direct Recruitment
Livestock Extension Officer (Animal Husbandry Dept.) – For Departmental Employees only

selection_process: Subject Type Total Questions Maximum Marks Duration
Biology, Agriculture and Animal Husbandry (जीव विज्ञान, कृषि एवं पशुपालन) Objective MCQ (OMR/CBT) 100 100 2 Hours
1st Tie-Break Higher age (older candidate preferred — senior in age gets priority)
2nd Tie-Break Alphabetical order of names (in English alphabet)

The selection process consists of an Objective Type (MCQ) Written Examination of 100 marks and 2 hours duration. The questions will be related to the educational qualification prescribed for the post. Document verification is mandatory after the written examination result. The final selection list will be published on the Commission's website.
Exam Mode: The Commission may conduct the examination in Offline (OMR-based) or Online (Computer Based Test / CBT) mode depending on the number of applicants.

OMR-based Exam Rules: OMR answer sheets are in triplicate. Candidates must mandatorily submit the original and second copy to the invigilator after the exam; the third copy may be retained. Use of whitener or scratching on OMR is strictly prohibited and will attract negative marking. Candidates must not write incorrect roll numbers or booklet series codes on the OMR sheet
else their candidature will be cancelled automatically.
Negative Marking: 1/4th (¼) marks will be deducted for each wrong answer. No marks will be deducted for unanswered questions. If a candidate marks more than one answer for a question
it will be treated as wrong and ¼ marks will be deducted.
The seniority list will be prepared based on written exam marks combined with applicable horizontal reservation and vertical reservation norms as per Uttarakhand Govt. service rules.

Document Verification (अभिलेख सन्निरीक्षा): Document verification is a mandatory stage after written exam results. All reservation/educational/experience/desirable qualification claims must be supported by valid certificates as per service rules. Candidates absent during document verification will have their candidature cancelled. All information regarding document verification will be communicated via the UKSSSC website only.

syllabus.details: Written Examination – One Paper: Biology, Agriculture and Animal Husbandry (100 Marks, 100 Questions, 2 Hours) | Note: Part A is compulsory for all candidates. Part B (Biology – Botany and Zoology) and Part C (Agriculture and Animal Husbandry) are optional; candidates must choose any one of Part B or C. | Part A – Compulsory for All (30 Marks) | Part B – Biology (Botany and Zoology) – 70 Marks [OPTIONAL – any one of B or C] | Part C – Agriculture and Animal Husbandry – 70 Marks [OPTIONAL – any one of B or C] | NOTE: Current General Knowledge and scientific advancement related to the above units are also deemed to be included in the syllabus.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the UKSSSC portal www.sssc.uk.gov.in. No manual or postal applications will be accepted. The online portal will be active from 13 May 2026 to 02 June 2026. A correction window will be available from 05 June 2026 to 07 June 2026.
Online Application Process – Step by Step:
At time of uploading the online application, the following must be uploaded along with:
High School (10th) certificate — for name, father's name and date of birth proof (only HS marksheet accepted for DOB; no other document valid)
Intermediate (12th) / Graduation marksheets and certificates related to educational qualification
Aadhaar Card (both sides) — mandatory for identity
Caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) as applicable — for Uttarakhand residents only
Employment Office Registration Certificate (from Uttarakhand Employment Office)
EWS Income and Property Certificate (issued between 01-04-2026 and 02-06-2026 / last date of application; valid for FY 2025-26 and applicable for FY 2026-27)
OBC certificate — must be issued within 3 years of the last date of application

Documents to be produced in original at Document Verification: All certificates uploaded online must be presented in original along with self-attested copies at the time of document verification when called by the Commission.
important_instructions: Online applications only — no manual or postal applications will be accepted. Candidates must ensure they fulfill all eligibility conditions (educational qualification, age, domicile, employment registration) before applying. Admission at all stages is provisional and candidature can be cancelled even after final selection if found ineligible. Candidates must not fill more than one online application form for the same post. If multiple applications are submitted, all will be cancelled. Candidates can cancel and re-apply before the deadline, but fee paid for a cancelled application will not be refunded or adjusted. All information filled in the online application is treated as final and confirmed. Incorrect or false information / fake certificates will result in debarment from all UKSSSC examinations and FIR under applicable laws. Signatures on the application, attendance sheet, document verification, and other correspondence must be identical at all places — any discrepancy may result in cancellation of candidature. Each candidate must provide their own separate mobile number and email ID — not shared with other family members. Admit cards will not be sent by post — they must be downloaded from the UKSSSC website. Candidates must regularly check the website throughout the recruitment process. Mobile phones, calculators, pagers, scanners, or any electronic communication devices are strictly prohibited inside the examination hall. Violation will attract debarment from future UKSSSC exams and further action. Examination centre preferences are taken into account, but the Commission's decision on exam centre allotment is final and binding. Candidates absent during document verification will have their candidature cancelled without any notice. All Uttarakhand State Agitation Activist / dependant certificates must be claimed in the online application form to avail reservation; no benefit is available if not claimed at application stage. No TA/DA is payable to any candidate for attending written examination or document verification. Any person with more than one living spouse, or a woman who has married a person with a living spouse, is not eligible for appointment. All decisions of the Commission regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding. The Commission does not provide individual counselling on eligibility.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies for UKSSSC Pashudhan Prasar Adhikari 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 120 vacancies have been announced — 108 posts are for direct recruitment (सीधी भर्ती) and 12 posts are reserved exclusively for departmental employees (विभागीय कार्मिकों) of the Animal Husbandry Department, Uttarakhand. The number of vacancies is subject to change based on revised requisitions from the concerned department.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required to apply for Pashudhan Prasar Adhikari?
faq.answer: For direct recruitment candidates, a Bachelor's Degree in any one of the following subjects from a university established by law in India is required: Biology (जीव विज्ञान), Agriculture (कृषि), or Animal Husbandry (पशुपालन). For departmental employees applying under the 12 reserved posts, Intermediate (10+2) with Biology or Agriculture subjects along with 5 years of continuous departmental service is sufficient.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for UKSSSC Pashudhan Prasar Adhikari 2026, and what is the cut-off date?
faq.answer: The decisive cut-off date for age calculation is 01 July 2026. Candidates must be minimum 21 years and maximum 42 years of age as on this date, meaning they must have been born not after 01 July 2005 and not before 02 July 1984. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for SC/ST/OBC (Uttarakhand), 5 years for Freedom Fighter dependants, and 10 years for Persons with Disabilities (Uttarakhand) for Group-C posts. Ex-servicemen get relaxation equal to their defence service period (resultant age not to exceed prescribed upper limit by more than 3 years).

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee, and is it refundable?
faq.answer: The examination fee is Rs. 300/- for Unreserved / OBC candidates, Rs. 150/- for Uttarakhand SC/ST/EWS and Divyang candidates, and Rs. 00/- (Nil) for Orphan (Anath) candidates. Fee must be paid online via Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, or UPI. The fee is non-refundable under any circumstances. Bank drafts and cheques are not accepted.

faq.question: Q5. What is the exam pattern and syllabus for the Pashudhan Prasar Adhikari written examination?
faq.answer: The written examination consists of one objective type (MCQ) paper of 100 questions carrying 100 marks, to be completed in 2 hours. The paper is divided into three parts: Part A (Biology – 30 marks, compulsory for all), and Part B (Biology – Botany and Zoology, 70 marks) or Part C (Agriculture and Animal Husbandry, 70 marks) — candidates must attempt any one of Part B or C. There is negative marking of ¼ (0.25) marks for each wrong answer. The exam may be conducted in offline OMR mode or online CBT mode.

faq.question: Q6. Is Uttarakhand domicile mandatory to apply? Can candidates from other states apply?
faq.answer: These posts are for candidates applying under Group-C direct recruitment outside the purview of Uttarakhand Public Service Commission. To apply, at least one of the following conditions must be met: (a) registered at an Employment Office in Uttarakhand, OR (b) holding a Permanent/Original Resident Certificate of Uttarakhand. Candidates from other states may apply if they hold Uttarakhand domicile. However, reservation benefits (SC/ST/OBC/EWS, women, ex-serviceman, etc.) are available only to permanent/original residents of Uttarakhand.

faq.question: Q7. What are the minimum qualifying marks required to pass the written examination?
faq.answer: Candidates belonging to Unreserved (UR), OBC, and EWS categories must score a minimum of 45% marks in the written examination. Candidates belonging to SC and ST categories must score a minimum of 35% marks. Candidates who do not meet these minimum qualifying marks will not be considered in the written competitive examination and will be disqualified from further stages.

faq.question: Q8. What happens after the written examination? Is there an interview or physical test?
faq.answer: There is no interview or physical test in this recruitment. After the written examination result is declared, candidates in the merit list will be called for mandatory Document Verification (अभिलेख सन्निरीक्षा). All reservation/qualification claims are verified at this stage. Candidates who fail to appear for document verification or whose documents are found incorrect/invalid will have their candidature cancelled. The final selection list will be published on the UKSSSC website after document verification is complete.
